text: Hi, it's Carla Gannett from Tourism Saskatchewan in an ice fishing tent on
Last Mountain Lake here with Jeff Mattity, local Last Mountain Lake expert.
Well thanks for saying that. There's a lot more skilled experts I guess on
the water here but yes I've been privileged to be able to outfit here
with G&S Marine Outfitters as a guide for them for just about a decade I guess
now. Last Mountain Lake is a glacial cut prairie gem and it's been here since the
last Ice Age and because of that it is a large lake. It has a lot of diversity of
species which means there's a lot of forage for all species at all ages of
their life cycle.
There's a lot of different species that are here.
There's a lot of different species that are here.
Solid one. And all the walleye. Anything that just shows up up here is a thick mark.
It's probably going to be a perch or a cisco. The ones that rise off the bottom
are generally going to be the walleye.
Or if it's on the edge of the cone or if it's in the middle of the cone.
We are sitting on Last Mountain Lake just outside of Regina with Tom Gruenwald
from Tom Gruenwald Outdoors. Welcome to Saskatchewan Tom.
Well hey it's great to be here Carla. That's great. So this is not your first
time in the province is it? No we've actually been up here now a couple times
with the guys. We went up to Tobin Lake three years ago. Spent a couple of days
up there with them. Had a great time. Came back went to Lac La Plange last year. Fished here.
Last year. Fished here. Last year. We went up to Tobin Lake three years ago. Spent a couple of days up there with them. Had a great time. Came back. Went to Lac La Plange last year. Fished here.
Last year. Fished here. Last year. Fishing lake trout and now here we are on Last Mountain Lake.
Saskatchewan is a place to be for ice fishing. Oh it's a it's a destination
for me. I mean you've got trophy walleyes, trophy pike. The lake trout fishing is
second to none. I mean what we experienced on Lac La Plange last year
was just phenomenal and you got such friendly people here in this province. I
mean every time I've been up here we had just a wonderful time out on the ice. And
you have no idea just how special it is the lakes here are so fertile and you
have so many fishers,
that are available to ice fishermen. I think definitely, I mean, if people want to come,
if you're interested in trophy walleyes, if you're interested in trophy pike, if you're
interested in trophy lake trout, this is a place to come. And even for smaller fish, if you want
perch or cisco, you know, anything is just, it's such a variety of species and it's such a great
place to come. I recommend it to anybody. What are you fishing for today? Today we're mostly
fishing for trophy walleyes. We're also fishing some tip-ups for pike as well. And we've been
lucky. We've been picking up some suspended fish. There's some ciscos and even some perch coming
through. So a variety of species. Excellent. And so where are you from actually? I'm from Campbell
Sport, Wisconsin, which is right between Milwaukee and Green Bay. So it's a little bit of a ride to
come up here. The first time we actually drove, believe it or not, a friend and I. So it took us
two days to get up here. But the last couple of times now we've flown into Saskatoon and then from
there we drove to our destination. Saskatchewan here, I really had a terrific time on Lac La
Plange fishing those lake trout last year.
And the
opportunity here, I'm shaking every time I put my lure in the water because I know there's 13 and 14
pound walleyes here.
